output: Implemented batch 1 / T1 by adding a shared mobile interaction policy model, threading it through `useTimelineState` into the split editor data/ops contexts and `VideoEditorProvider`, and expanding provider coverage so consumers can read/update the policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without calling `useTimelineState()` directly. `interactionStateRef` remains the only save/poll deferral gate. Checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_1.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `mobile-interaction-model` and threaded `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `precisionEnabled`, `contextTarget`, and `inspectorTarget` through `useTimelineState()` into the split editor data/ops contexts and `VideoEditorProvider`. Provider coverage now proves consumers can read and update the policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without bypassing the provider. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`. `interactionStateRef` remains the only save/poll gate because `useTimelineSave()` still receives only that ref and no second persistence suppression path was added.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The interaction policy is now published on `useTimelineEditorData()` and updated through `useTimelineEditorOps()` via `VideoEditorProvider`, so shell, timeline, preview, and properties consumers do not need to call `useTimelineState()` directly. `interactionStateRef` is still the sole save/poll gate because the persistence path still only passes that ref into `useTimelineSave()` and the new policy state does not participate in save suppression.
